perturbation.original_text: The schedule set forth in Exhibit A will constitute invoicing for the events. Notwithstanding the above, Torvec may, in its sole discretion, cancel its participation in any of the above-referenced events by notifying Vendor of such cancellation not later than two weeks prior to the due date(s) for payment hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the above, Torvec may, in its sole discretion, cancel its participation in any of the above-referenced events by notifying Vendor of such cancellation not later than two weeks prior to the due date(s) for payment hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'The schedule set forth in Exhibit A will constitute invoicing for the events.', the contract now lacks a clear mechanism for invoicing. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 5, which states 'As consideration for the benefits it receives under this Agreement, Torvec shall provide the following to Vendor: See Exhibit A'. While Exhibit A exists and outlines benefits, the direct link between the schedule and invoicing is severed, potentially leading to disputes about how and when Vendor will be paid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment and Other Consideration As consideration for the benefits it receives under this Agreement, Torvec shall provide the following to Vendor: See Exhibit A Notwithstanding the above, Torvec may, in its sole discretion, cancel its participation in any of the above-referenced events by notifying Vendor of such cancellation not later than two weeks prior to the due date(s) for payment hereunder. All checks shall be made payabe to: Phoenix Performance, LLC and mailed to 481, Schuylkill Rd, Phoenixville, Pa. 19460. (b) Equipment
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that "The schedule set forth in Exhibit A will constitute invoicing for the events." This directly links Exhibit A to the invoicing process. The changed text omits this crucial statement, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states, "As consideration for the benefits it receives under this Agreement, Torvec shall provide the following to Vendor: See Exhibit A." Without the initial statement, the connection between Exhibit A and invoicing is severed, making it unclear how Torvec will fulfill its payment obligations as described in the contradicted text. This omission introduces ambiguity and undermines the enforceability of the payment terms, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor shall maintain insurance for not less than the following limits and coverage with duly licensed insurance companies having an A.M. Best rating of A-, X or better. In addition to covering all of the normal operations of Vendor, this insurance shall cover all of the activities and events described under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor shall maintain insurance to cover all of the activities and events described under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'for not less than the following limits and coverage with duly licensed insurance companies having an A.M. Best rating of A-, X or better. In addition to covering all of the normal operations of Vendor, this insurance' removes the specified insurance requirements. This weakens the enforceability of the insurance clause, potentially leaving Torvec vulnerable if Vendor carries inadequate insurance. The removal creates an in-text contradiction, because section 8 is requiring the insurance, but it no longer specifies coverage and limits.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Insurance Vendor shall maintain insurance to cover all of the activities and events described under this Agreement. • General Liability: Vendor shall maintain commercial general liability (CGL) insurance with a limit of not less than $1 million each occurrence. CGL coverages shall be written on ISO occurrence form CG 00 01 or a substitute form providing equivalent coverage and shall cover liabilities arising from events, premises, operations, independent contractors, products-completed operations, personal injury and advertising injury, and liability assumed under an insured contract. Subaru of America, Inc., its parent and subsidiaries shall be included as additional insureds under the CGL using ISO additional insured endorsement CG 20 10 or a substitute providing equivalent coverage.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified minimum insurance limits and coverage requirements, including A.M. Best ratings. The changed text omits these specific requirements, stating only that the insurance must cover all activities and events described in the agreement. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which lists specific insurance requirements, such as commercial general liability (CGL) insurance with a limit of not less than $1 million each occurrence. The omission in the changed text weakens the enforceability of the insurance clause, potentially leaving Torvec vulnerable if Vendor carries inadequate insurance. Therefore, the removal directly contradicts the detailed insurance specifications provided in the 'Contradicted Text'.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that each of them hereby submits to the jurisdiction of the New York State and federal courts for the purpose of resolving any dispute arising under this Agreement and that the exclusive venue for resolution of such disputes shall be state or federal courts located in Monroe County, New York.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that each of them hereby submits to the jurisdiction for the purpose of resolving any dispute arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'of the New York State and federal courts and that the exclusive venue for resolution of such disputes shall be state or federal courts located in Monroe County, New York', the contract now lacks a specified jurisdiction and venue for dispute resolution. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty, potentially leading to legal challenges about where disputes must be resolved. The in-text contradiction lies in stating there is an agreement to submit to a 'jurisdiction' without defining which one.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law, Jurisdiction and Venue This Agreement is to be governed and construed according to the laws of the State of New York without regard to conflicts of law. The parties agree that each of them hereby submits to the jurisdiction for the purpose of resolving any dispute arising under this Agreement and that the exclusive venue for resolution of such disputes shall be state or federal courts located in Monroe County, New York.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies New York State and federal courts in Monroe County, New York, as the exclusive jurisdiction and venue. The changed text omits this specification, stating only that the parties submit to 'the jurisdiction' without defining which one. This omission creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which expects a defined jurisdiction and venue. The changed text's ambiguity directly conflicts with the contradicted text's explicit specification, leading to uncertainty about where disputes should be resolved. This falls under the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language was removed creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.
